Off Calendar Jun-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Luke Garvey, esq., of Colla & Ray LLP. Tel:415-579-1414 for plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing is held. Plaintiff counsel contests the tentative ruling in lines 10, 4; 10, 5; and 10, 6. and Line 10, 2. granting a motion to compel rather than a motion to deem the truth of matters admitted. The matter argued. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified:

2 - PLAINTIFF JOHN MUHAWIEH's Motion To Deem The Truth Of Matters Specified In Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted And Request For Sanctions.

The Court deems the Requests for Admission admitted and strikes the required responses.

Plaintiff Counsel Luke Garvey to prepare proposed order and email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff John Muhawieh To Compel Defendant Seamus Collin'S Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Samuel Ray
Off Calendar Jun-20-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Luke Garvey, esq., of Colla & Ray LLP. Tel:415-579-1414 for plaintiff.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing is held. The matter not argued/contested. The court adopts its tentative ruling

3 - PLAINTIFF JOHN MUHAWIEH AN INDIVIDUAL's Motion Of Plaintiff John Muhawieh To Compel Defendant Seamus Collin'S Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions.

Granted as to the motion to compel and granted in part as to the request for monetary sanctions. No opposition filed and good cause shown. Defendant must serve verified code-compliant responses without objections, produce all responsive documents, and pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $800 (2 hour of attorney time at $400 per hour) no later than July 8, 2025.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem The Truth Of Matters Specified In Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - PLAINTIFF JOHN MUHAWIEH's Motion To Deem The Truth Of Matters Specified In Request For Admissions, Set One, Admitted And Request For Sanctions.

Moving party shall provide a proposed order on EACH motion.

The motion was not served timely and, thus, is ordered OFF CALENDAR.

To be heard today, a motion must have been filed and served 16 court days before today, i.e., April 11, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b).) Where, as here, the moving papers are served electronically, two court days are added to the service timeline. Thus, to be timely electronically served, Plaintiff needed to serve the moving papers by April 9, 2025. Plaintiff, however, served the moving papers on April 10, 2025-too late to be noticed for hearing today. Defendants have not filed oppositions; thus, the court cannot find forfeiture of the issue or a lack of prejudice. Accordingly, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Thus, to be timely electronically served, Plaintiff needed to serve the moving papers by April 9, 2025. Plaintiff, however, served the moving papers on April 10, 2025-too late to be noticed for hearing today. Defendants have not filed oppositions; thus, the court cannot find forfeiture of the issue or a lack of prejudice. Accordingly, the motion is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
